title: Phlomoides sewerzovii
taxonomy: Phlomoides sewerzovii is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to China, specifically in the Sichuan province. The classification of Phlomoides sewerzovii includes being a member of the genus Phlomoides, which is a group of plants that were previously classified as part of the genus Phlomis. The genus Phlomoides is closely related to the genus Phlomis and is part of the subfamily Lamioideae. The plant's classification also places it within the family Lamiaceae, which includes many other plants commonly known as mint or sage.

intro: Phlomoides sewerzovii, also known as Sewertzoff's phlomoides, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its use in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Phlomoides sewerzovii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Phlomoides sewerzovii is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared by steeping the plant's leaves in hot water or mixing them with a solvent to create a concentrated extract.
